guideline_references: Burch-Wartofsky Point Scale for thyroid storm
American Thyroid Association Guidelines for the Diagnosis and Management of Hyperthyroidism 2016
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who palpated a goitre, auscultated a thyroid bruit, tested reflexes and measured vitals, and coordinated a Priority 1 ambulance. The AI Doctor CANNOT examine or administer IV drugs and is scored on recognising a hypermetabolic endocrine emergency, escalating to 000, and coaching the carer through the only patient-doable interventions (external cooling, low-stimulation environment, do not restrain).
Age inferred as adult (~45) from a married patient with established Graves' disease on carbimazole/propranolol; no age stated in the source — flagged.

Anchoring / premature-closure trap: a preceding chest infection and high fever invite an anchoring diagnosis of sepsis; the tachycardia out of proportion to the fever, the goitre/tremor/hyperreflexia and the abrupt anti-thyroid drug cessation point to thyroid storm (with the infection as the precipitant, not the whole story).

Errors-of-commission probe: salicylate antipyretics are contraindicated (they displace thyroid hormone from binding proteins and worsen the crisis); the correct antipyretic pathway is external cooling plus paracetamol.
Source category: AUC (Acute Urgent Care), source note AUC-049.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
